idea.hook: What if Earth was in a giant void?
facts: The observable universe has a giant void spanning 1.3 billion light-years, roughly 8,000 times the distance from New York to Los Angeles
This void is nearly the size of 250,000,000,000 Texas-sized areas
If Earth were at its center, the nearest galaxy would be 200 million light-years away, or 1,500 times the distance to Andromeda
idea.payoff: and we still don't know why it exists
idea.title: Earth in a Void? 🌌
idea.description: Imagine being in a vast, seemingly endless space with no stars or galaxies nearby 🌌. The giant void in our universe is a mysterious region that's left scientists puzzled. Dive into the mind-blowing facts about this enormous expanse and its potential implications for our understanding of the cosmos 🚀

scenes.text: What if Earth was in a GIANT VOID?
scenes.visual: A small Earth graphic slowly shrinks and zooms out, revealing vast, dark, empty space around it.
An expanding dark void with faint, barely visible galaxies at its extreme edges. Text fades in over this visual.
A stylized 'Texas' shape appears, then rapidly multiplies and fills the screen, shrinking to represent the massive scale, then transitions back to a void.

scenes.text: Nearest galaxy: 200 MILLION light-years away (1500x Andromeda!)
scenes.visual: A tiny Earth dot at the center of an immense, empty expanse. A faint, distant spiral galaxy twinkles at the absolute edge of the screen, emphasizing isolation.

scenes.text: And WE STILL DON'T KNOW WHY IT EXISTS.
scenes.visual: The dark, featureless void slowly spins, appearing infinite and mysterious. The emptiness is profound and unsettling.
